title: # Start Kernel Orchestration Start Kernel orchestration for the current task inside this Codex session. Execute immediately. Do not create or edit GitHub issues, pull requests, review comments, or issue comments. Do not inspect CI unless explicitly asked later. Backup-only GitHub Actions dispatch or...
summary_text: [phase:critique] [family:smoke-contract] [provider_hint:codex] Findings: - The bootstrap timeout contract is stricter than the bridge contract for Claude. Launcher hard-caps every bootstrap lane at `30s` in [codex-prompt-launch](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/bin/codex-prompt-launch#L446), while bridge default for Claude is `180000ms` in [kernel-lane-bridge.mjs](/Users/masayuki/Dev/kernel-orchestration-tools/kernel-lane-bridge.mjs#L64). This mismatch is the most likely cause of Claude cold-start false negatives. - Retry shape assumes identical latency each attempt (`3` attempts, fixed timeout, fixed backoff) in [codex-prompt-launch](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/bin/codex-prompt-launch#L532). For Claude, cold-start variance is high, so fixed `30s` repeats the same failure mode. - Smoke harness budget is tight relative to bootstrap retry behavior: `120s` global timeout in [check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh#L12). If Claude needs >30s, bootstrap can fail before ack or consume most of the run budget. - Guard settings are not the primary bottleneck here. The failure happens before guard lane-watch enforcement; guard’s quorum startup floor (`15s`) in [session_watch.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/session_watch.py#L705) is downstream. Smallest safe change: - Add a Claude-specific bootstrap timeout override in launcher (keep others unchanged), e.g. in [codex-prompt-launch](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/bin/codex-prompt-launch#L446): use `KERNEL_BOOTSTRAP_CLAUDE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS` (default `60`) when `provider=claude`, otherwise keep current `30`. - Why this is smallest/safest: one-file, minimal blast radius, preserves fail-closed semantics, and directly addresses the known mismatch causing false timeouts. Test gap to add immediately after: - Launcher regression proving provider-specific timeout selection (`claude=60`, `glm=30`) in [test-codex-prompt-launcher.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/tests/test-codex-prompt-launcher.sh).
